skill.name: contribute-catalog
skill.description: Guide contributors through creating HyperFrames registry blocks or components and preparing an upstream catalog pull request.
skill.summary: Create and submit HyperFrames catalog blocks and components with templates, validation, previews, and PR steps.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby or shell backtick hits, weak cryptography hits, reconnaissance hits, and hardcoded URLs. Review found the backtick and weak-crypto signals are Markdown examples or ordinary template text, but the skill does intentionally guide external CLI commands, file changes, publishing, git pushes, and PR creation. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found, so publication is acceptable with a medium operational-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: External Command and Publishing Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run HyperFrames, npx, git, and GitHub CLI commands for linting, rendering, publishing, committing, pushing, and opening a pull request. This is legitimate for a catalog-contribution skill, but it can modify a repository and publish artifacts, so agents should request user approval before running commands that write, publish, push, or create PRs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command workflow is explicit and semantically required by the skill. It is not malicious, but it has clear side effects in the local repository and remote services.

medium_findings.title: Internal Upload Script Requires Cloud Credentials
medium_findings.description: The skill tells internal contributors to run an image upload script that requires a specific AWS profile. This appears to be a legitimate internal publishing path, but it should be limited to authorized contributors and should not be run automatically.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly names an upload script and required AWS profile. The risk depends on contributor authorization, so the confidence is high for operational risk but not for malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Third-Party CDN and Font Dependencies in Templates
low_findings.description: The starter templates load Google Fonts, GSAP, and Three.js from public CDN URLs. These are normal browser dependencies for visual templates, but generated catalog items will perform network requests unless contributors vendor or pin dependencies differently.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are visible in template HTML and are common public dependencies. No credential transfer or suspicious destination was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are caused by Markdown code fences and inline code examples. The reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings do not correspond to actual cryptographic or reconnaissance behavior in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation, JSON examples, HTML examples, or ordinary descriptive text. I found no executable Ruby backticks, weak cryptographic algorithm use, or system probing logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Commands with Local and Remote Side Effects
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes commands that render output, publish previews, stage files, commit, push, and create a GitHub pull request. These should be treated as user-approved actions, even though they fit the purpose of the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The side-effecting commands are directly present in the documented workflow. Their purpose is clear, and there is no evidence of stealth or exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Browser Assets in Generated Templates
dangerous_patterns.description: The templates include public CDN scripts and font assets. This creates a supply-chain and network dependency for rendered catalog items, but the endpoints are recognizable public library providers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network dependency is explicit in HTML templates. No hidden destination, credential collection, or obfuscation was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent registry entries when adding reusable video blocks or components. This skill guides scaffolding, template selection, validation, preview generation, and pull request preparation.
actual_capabilities: Clarifies whether a contribution should be a registry block or a reusable component.
Provides required folder layouts and registry metadata patterns for blocks and components.
Supplies starter templates for captions, VFX blocks, registry metadata, and overlay components.
Lists validation, preview, formatting, documentation, and publication commands for contributors.
Defines quality gates for timeline behavior, ID prefixes, screenshots, docs pages, and PR readiness.
limitations: It is specific to the HyperFrames repository and registry structure.
It does not replace maintainer review or catalog acceptance decisions.
Some publishing steps require GitHub access, HyperFrames tooling, or internal cloud credentials.
Templates still require contributor design work, testing, and final asset generation.

use_cases.title: Add a Caption Style
use_cases.description: Create a caption block with readable typography, timed karaoke highlighting, deterministic animation, and registry metadata.

use_cases.title: Publish a VFX Block
use_cases.description: Build a Three.js visual effect that follows HyperFrames timeline rules and passes catalog validation.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Catalog Pull Request
use_cases.description: Run the required checks, generate previews and docs, commit the registry files, and open a reviewable PR.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Contribution
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to contribute a HyperFrames catalog item. Help me decide whether it should be a block or a component, then list the files I need.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Caption Block
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me scaffold a new caption block named [name]. Use the catalog rules for IDs, timing, typography, registry metadata, and validation.

prompt_templates.title: Build a VFX Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a HyperFrames VFX block using Three.js. Include deterministic animation, timeline update rendering, registry metadata, and preview steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit PR Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my HyperFrames catalog contribution for PR readiness. Check file placement, registry metadata, generated docs, previews, validation commands, and final git steps.

output_examples.input: A contributor wants to add a bold social caption style.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies it as a block, recommends a prefixed folder name, lists the required HTML and registry metadata files, and provides validation and preview steps.

output_examples.input: A motion designer wants to submit a reusable overlay.
output_examples.output: The skill classifies it as a component, explains that it should avoid fixed duration, and guides transparent background and prefixed class naming.

output_examples.input: A finished registry item needs review before submission.
output_examples.output: The skill checks linting, validation, formatting, docs generation, preview media, registry registration, commit preparation, and pull request requirements.

best_practices: Ask the user to approve commands that publish, push, commit, or create a pull request.
Keep every generated ID and class prefix unique to prevent composition collisions.
Run validation, preview, and documentation generation before opening the PR.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for installing existing catalog items into a project.
Do not run internal upload scripts unless the user is authorized and has the required profile.
Do not submit a catalog entry without preview media, registry registration, and generated docs.

faq.question: What should I use this skill for?
faq.answer: Use it to create a new HyperFrames registry block or component and prepare an upstream catalog pull request.

faq.question: Does it install existing catalog items?
faq.answer: No. It is for contributions to the public catalog, not for installing existing registry items.

faq.question: Can it create both blocks and components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains the difference and provides separate structure and metadata guidance for each type.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It documents commands for validation, preview, publishing, git, and PR creation. Users should approve side-effecting commands first.

faq.question: Are internal publishing steps required?
faq.answer: No. External contributors attach preview media to the PR, while maintainers handle catalog image upload.

faq.question: What tools does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects the HyperFrames CLI, Node tooling, git, and optionally the GitHub CLI for pull request creation.
